zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Barber於1486年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可福雷辛費爾德時，他的父親Sir Nicholas Barber26歲，母親Lady Catherine de Thiesselin26歲。他於1503年4月29日與Lady Alice Bohun在英國素福爾可福雷辛費爾德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1558年1月6日在他的家鄉享年72歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可福雷辛費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir James Barber于1486年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可福雷辛費爾德时，他的父亲Sir Nicholas Barber26岁，母亲Lady Catherine de Thiesselin26岁。他于1503年4月29日与Lady Alice Bohun在英国素福爾可福雷辛費爾德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1558年1月6日在他的家乡享年72岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可福雷辛費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Barber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a barber, from Middle English barb(o)ur ‘barber’ (Anglo-Norman French barber, Old French barbier, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). In the Middle Ages barbers not only cut hair and shaved beards, but also practised surgery and pulled teeth.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name from German Barbier ‘barber’ (compare 1 above).
Catalan: occupational name for a barber, barber (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Berber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Salvador, Guillermo, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Luis, Adriana, Alfredo, Angelina, Armando, Carlos.
Catalan: probably a variant of   Barber  . This surname is most common in Mexico.
Turkish: occupational name from berber ‘barber’.

variants.name: Barbero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ricardo, Alvino, Ana, Angel, Arsenio, Fernando, Gustavo, Jaime, Jose, Maribel, Mario, Nenita.  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Domenic, Ernani, Giulio, Italo.  French Emile, Yves.

Spanish and Italian (Piedmont): occupational name for a barber-surgeon, barbero, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’ (from Latin barba); see also Barber . This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Barbieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Angelo, Vito, Rocco, Aldo, Cosmo, Dino, Domenic, Geno, Giovanna, Guido.
Italian: occupational name from barbiere ‘barber-surgeon’ (see Barber ), from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbour
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: occupational name from Old French barbeor ‘barber’. Compare the English form Barber .

History: George Barbour sailed from England on the Transport in 1635. He became a freeman of Dedham, MA, in 1647 and settled eventually at Medfield. A Scottish merchant named Barbour settled in eastern VA in the latter half of the 17th century.

variants.name: Borbely
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Antal, Ferenc, Ildiko, Kalman, Laszlo, Miklos.
Hungarian (Borbély): occupational name for a barber, Hungarian borbély. See also Barber .

variants.name: Barbeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Monique, Alcide, Alphonse, Amiee, Armand, Gisele, Jean Francois, Lucien, Lucienne, Michel.
French:

from barbeau ‘barbel’, a type of fish, hence a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, or a nickname for a man with a sparse beard, the fish being distinguished by beardlike growths on either side of its mouth.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Barber was born in 1486, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Nicholas Barber, was 26 and his mother, Lady Catherine de Thiesselin, was 26. He married Lady Alice Bohun on 29 April 1503, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 6 January 1558, in his hometown, at the age of 72, and was buried in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Barber est né en 1486, à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Nicholas Barber, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lady Catherine de Thiesselin, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Lady Alice Bohun le 29 avril 1503, à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 6 janvier 1558, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James Barber 1486 in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Nicholas Barber 26 und seine Mutter Lady Catherine de Thiesselin 26. Er hat am 29. April 1503 in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England Lady Alice Bohun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 6. Januar 1558 im Alter von 72 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Barber è nato il nel 1486 nel Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Nicholas Barber, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Lady Catherine de Thiesselin, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Lady Alice Bohun il 29 aprile 1503, nel Fressingfield, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 6 gennaio 1558, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Barberが1486年にイギリスイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドで生まれた時，父Sir Nicholas Barberは26歳，母Lady Catherine de Thiesselinは26歳であった。1503年4月29日にイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドでLady Alice Bohunと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1558年1月6日に故郷で72歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Barber(이)가 1486년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 프레징필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Nicholas Barber(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Catherine de Thiesselin(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1503년 4월 29일 영국 수폴크 프레징필드에서 Lady Alice Bohun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1558년 1월 6일 그의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 프레징필드에 묻혔다.

pt.BET 11 JAN 1508 AND 10 JAN 1509: de 11 de janeiro de 1508 a 10 de janeiro de 1509
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Barber nasceu em 1486, em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Nicholas Barber, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lady Catherine de Thiesselin, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Alice Bohun em 29 de abril de 1503, em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de janeiro de 1558, em sua cidade natal, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Barber родился в 1486 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Nicholas Barber было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Catherine de Thiesselin, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice Bohun 29 апреля 1503 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 6 января 1558 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir James Barber nació en 1486, en Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Nicholas Barber, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lady Catherine de Thiesselin, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Lady Alice Bohun el 29 de abril de 1503, en Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 6 de enero de 1558, en su pueblo, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
